free_text: Activation cross-section measurement of deuteron
induced reactions on cerium for biomedical
applications and for development of reaction theory
Natural metallic cerium foils.
Two stacks were
irradiated with deuteron beams of 50 and 21 MeV.
Stack irradiated at LLN, Louvain la Neuve:
Incident energy: 50 MeV
Beam intensity: 136 nA
Irradiation time: 38 min
Stack composition: Ce(72.7 micron), target
Number of target foils: 30
Al(27 micron), monitor and energy
degrader
Cooling times: 4.5 h, 47 h and 335 h
Stack was irradiated at VUB, Brussels:
Incident energy: 21 MeV
Beam intensity: 187 nA
Irradiation time: 60 min
Stack composition: Ce(25 micron), target
Number of target foils: 18
Ti(10.9 micron), monitor
Al(26.7 micron), energy degrader
Cooling times: 1.1 h, 45 h and 335 h
Nuclear data were taken from NUDAT.
The uncertainty on the median energy in the
foils includes the cumulative effects of uncertainty of
incident beam energy, uncertainty of stopping power of
each foil due to thickness variation and straggling
effects.
The uncertainty on the cross section values
are estimated in the standard way following the
recommendations of the ISO-guide as the square root of
the quadratic summation of the independently
contributing parameters. Uncertainty of time parameters
is not included.
beam current(7%)
beam loss correction (1.5%)
target thickness (3%)
detector efficiency (5%)
statistical and peak fitting (1-20%)
The experimental data were compared to the
results of the theoretical model code TALYS, ALICE-D
and EMPIRE-D.
The measured experimental data were compared to
those found in the literature.
Fitted excitation functions were used for
determination of integral physical yields.
VS.  SF5: M+ deleted in 002.
